Codex Rehome's first alpha release provides defensive inspection, planning, remapping, same-filesystem moving, verification, and rollback for the locally validated Codex CLI 0.144.x state shape.

This is a prerelease. Use it only with synthetic or disposable projects. Codex local state is an undocumented internal format, and writes fail closed when the exact schema adapter does not match.

Highlights:

- deterministic plans with separate migration IDs;
- SQLite online backups, hashes, restricted permissions, and exact rollback verification;
- structured rollout and Desktop-state rewriting without arbitrary prose replacement;
- interruption and recovery testing, including WAL and atomic-file replacement cases;
- content-free compatibility diagnostics through `codex-rehome doctor`;
- macOS arm64/x86_64 and Linux x86_64 archives with SHA-256 sidecars;
- Bash, Fish, PowerShell, and Zsh completions plus a man page.

Before any write, close Codex Desktop and every Codex CLI process, run `doctor`, review `inspect` and `plan`, and keep the generated backup until recovery is confirmed.
